message:
feat: Initialize Rust project with Cargo configuration

Add the foundational Cargo project setup for caut (Coding Agent Usage
Tracker), a cross-platform CLI tool for monitoring LLM provider usage.

Cargo.toml configuration:
- Package: caut v0.1.0, Rust 2024 edition, requires Rust 1.88+
- Binary target: caut (src/main.rs entry point)
- License: MIT, repository: github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_usage_tracker

Core dependencies:
- CLI: clap 4.5 with derive and env features, clap_complete for shell completions
- Serialization: serde 1.0 + serde_json + toml for config files
- Async: tokio (full) + reqwest 0.13 for HTTP with webpki-roots TLS
- Error handling: anyhow + thiserror for ergonomic error types
- Date/time: chrono with serde support
- Platform: directories for XDG paths, keyring for secure credential storage
- Terminal: rich_rust (local port of Python Rich), crossterm, colored
- Logging: tracing + tracing-subscriber with JSON output support
- Utilities: which for CLI detection, futures for async combinators

Build dependencies:
- vergen-gix 9.1 for embedding git SHA, build timestamp, and rustc version

Dev dependencies:
- Testing: tempfile, tokio-test, assert_cmd, predicates
- Validation: jsonschema for schema contract testing
- Mocking: wiremock for HTTP mocking in tests

Release profile optimized for binary size:
- opt-level = "z" (aggressive size optimization)
- LTO enabled, single codegen unit, panic = abort, stripped symbols

Lints:
- unsafe_code = "deny" (forbid unsafe except in tests for env manipulation)
- Clippy pedantic + nursery warnings enabled
- unused_async allowed for stub functions awaiting implementation

rust-toolchain.toml:
- Nightly channel required for Rust 2024 edition features
- Components: rustfmt, clippy

build.rs:
- Uses vergen-gix to emit build metadata at compile time
- Embeds: VERGEN_BUILD_TIMESTAMP, VERGEN_CARGO_TARGET_TRIPLE,
  VERGEN_GIT_SHA (short), VERGEN_GIT_DIRTY, VERGEN_RUSTC_SEMVER
- Enables --version to show detailed build information

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,88 @@
+[package]
+name = "caut"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+rust-version = "1.88"
+description = "Coding Agent Usage Tracker - CLI for monitoring LLM provider usage"
+license = "MIT"
+repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_usage_tracker"
+keywords = ["cli", "llm", "usage", "tracking", "codex", "claude"]
+categories = ["command-line-utilities"]
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "caut"
+path = "src/main.rs"
+
+[dependencies]
+# CLI argument parsing
+clap = { version = "4.5.54", features = ["derive", "env"] }
+clap_complete = "4.5.44"
+
+# Serialization
+serde = { version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"] }
+serde_json = "1.0.140"
+toml = "0.8"
+
+# Error handling
+anyhow = "1.0.100"
+thiserror = "2.0.17"
+
+# Async runtime + HTTP
+tokio = { version = "1.44", features = ["full"] }
+reqwest = { version = "0.13.1", features = ["json", "webpki-roots"] }
+
+# Date/time
+chrono = { version = "0.4.42", features = ["serde"] }
+
+# Platform paths and secrets
+directories = "6.0.0"
+keyring = { version = "3.6.3", features = ["apple-native", "windows-native", "linux-native"] }
+
+# Terminal output - using rich_rust (local port of Python Rich)
+rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", features = ["full"] }
+crossterm = "0.29.0"
+colored = "3.1.0"
+
+# Logging
+tracing = "0.1.41"
+tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3.19", features = ["env-filter", "json"] }
+which = "8.0.0"
+futures = "0.3.31"
+
+# Test utilities (optional)
+tempfile = { version = "3.15", optional = true }
+
+[build-dependencies]
+# Note: vergen-gix 9.1 requires Rust 1.88+; using 9.1 to match build.rs API
+vergen-gix = { version = "9.1", features = ["build", "cargo", "rustc"] }
+
+[features]
+default = []
+# Expose test utilities for integration tests
+test-utils = ["tempfile"]
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+tempfile = "3.15"
+jsonschema = "0.28"
+wiremock = "0.6"
+tokio-test = "0.4"
+assert_cmd = "2.0"
+predicates = "3.1"
+caut = { path = ".", features = ["test-utils"] }
+
+[profile.release]
+opt-level = "z"
+lto = true
+codegen-units = 1
+panic = "abort"
+strip = true
+
+[lints.rust]
+# deny (not forbid) allows tests to use #[allow(unsafe_code)] for env var manipulation
+unsafe_code = "deny"
+
+[lints.clippy]
+pedantic = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
+nursery = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
+# Allow async functions without await - stub functions will use async when implemented
+unused_async = "allow"
